config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters a lot, but individual rights should not be treated as secondary by default, because that is how governments and majorities can justify abuse. In my view, the best societies protect people while still allowing reasonable limits for public safety and the common good. From my perspective, rights are the foundation, and society is strongest when it respects them.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan atas hak individu, karena dari sudut pandang saya di Amerika Serikat, itu terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan oleh pemerintah atau mayoritas. Saya mengerti bahwa masyarakat butuh aturan bersama, tetapi hak individu bukan hiasan; hak itu justru batas yang mencegah keamanan publik berubah menjadi kontrol berlebihan. Menurut saya, yang benar adalah mencari keseimbangan: pembatasan boleh ada, tetapi harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau tidak, “kepentingan masyarakat” bisa menjadi alasan untuk mengorbankan orang yang paling rentan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective in Indonesia, I have seen how appeals to the "common good" can too easily be used to silence dissent, excuse corruption, or pressure minorities to stay quiet. Society does need order and shared responsibility, but that only works when individual rights are protected as a real limit on power, not treated as a luxury.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Sebagai orang dari Amerika Serikat, saya sangat curiga ketika “kepentingan umum” dipakai sebagai alasan untuk memberi negara atau mayoritas kekuasaan terlalu besar, karena sejarah kita sendiri menunjukkan betapa mudahnya itu bergeser jadi penyalahgunaan. Hak individu harus jadi batas utama, bukan penghalang yang bisa disapu begitu saja demi efisiensi atau ketertiban. Masyarakat memang perlu aturan bersama, tetapi prioritasnya harus keseimbangan yang ketat, bukan pengorbanan hak secara default.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Society should not take priority over individual rights by default, because in Indonesia that kind of language is often used to pressure ordinary people and protect the powerful. I have seen how “for the common good” can become a convenient excuse for silencing criticism, ignoring minorities, or demanding sacrifice from people who have the least power. Yes, public order and shared responsibility matter, but they must operate within clear limits so they do not turn into arbitrary control. A society is not truly healthy if it asks individuals to give up basic rights whenever the majority finds that easier.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan atas hak individu, karena dari pengalaman dan nilai yang kuat di Amerika Serikat, itu terlalu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran bagi kekuasaan yang berlebihan. Poin yang paling penting dari lawan diskusi adalah soal ketertiban umum, dan saya setuju ketertiban itu perlu, tetapi bukan dengan cara menjadikan hak sebagai sesuatu yang bisa dikesampingkan kapan saja oleh mayoritas. Hak individu harus menjadi batas yang nyata, karena tanpa batas itu, “demi masyarakat” sering kali hanya berarti orang yang paling lemah disuruh menanggung beban paling besar. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling patuh, melainkan yang paling mampu melindungi kebebasan sambil tetap membuat aturan yang sempit dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
